audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Numbers / Bemidbar supplementary offerings, equal-law, inadvertent/high-handed sin, Sabbath case, and tassels text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for future land offerings, grain/drink measures, sojourner equality, first dough, communal and individual inadvertent sin, defiant high-handed violation, Sabbath wood-gatherer execution, and tassels with blue cord. Strong anti-xenophobia, anti-legalism, and anti-violence safeguards required.

verses.restored: YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Speak to children of Israel and say to them: When you come into land of your dwellings that I am giving to you,
and you make fire-offering to YHWH, burnt offering or sacrifice, to fulfill special vow or as freewill offering or at your appointed times, to make soothing scent to YHWH from herd or flock,
then one bringing near his offering to YHWH shall bring near grain offering of one-tenth ephah fine flour mixed with fourth of a hin oil.
Wine for drink offering, fourth of a hin, you shall make with burnt offering or sacrifice for one lamb.
For a ram you shall make grain offering of two-tenths ephah fine flour mixed with third of a hin oil,
and wine for drink offering, third of a hin, you shall bring near as soothing scent to YHWH.
When you make son of herd as burnt offering or sacrifice to fulfill special vow or peace/well-being offerings to YHWH,
one shall bring with son of herd grain offering of three-tenths ephah fine flour mixed with half a hin oil,
and wine for drink offering, half a hin, as fire-offering of soothing scent to YHWH.
Thus it shall be done for each bull, or for each ram, or for lamb among sheep or goats.
According to number that you make, so you shall do for each according to their number.
Every native shall do these things thus, to bring near fire-offering of soothing scent to YHWH.
When a sojourner sojourns with you, or whoever is in your midst throughout generations, and makes fire-offering of soothing scent to YHWH, as you do, so he shall do.
For assembly, one statute shall be for you and for sojourner who sojourns, an age-long statute throughout generations; as you are, so shall sojourner be before YHWH.
One Torah/instruction and one judgment shall be for you and for sojourner who sojourns with you.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Speak to children of Israel and say to them: When you come into land where I bring you,
when you eat from bread of land, you shall lift up a contribution to YHWH.
First of your dough, a cake you shall lift up as contribution; as contribution of threshing floor, so you shall lift it.
From first of your dough you shall give to YHWH a contribution throughout generations.
If you err and do not do all these commands that YHWH spoke to Moses,
all that YHWH commanded you by hand of Moses, from day YHWH commanded and onward throughout generations,

if from eyes of congregation it was done in error, all congregation shall make one young bull as burnt offering, soothing scent to YHWH, with its grain offering and drink offering according to judgment, and one male goat as sin/purification offering.

Priest shall make atonement/covering for all congregation of children of Israel, and it shall be forgiven them, for error it was; they brought their offering, fire-offering to YHWH, and their sin/purification offering before YHWH for their error.
It shall be forgiven all congregation of children of Israel and sojourner sojourning in their midst, for in error all people were involved.
If one soul/life sins/misaligns in error, he shall bring female goat, daughter of a year, as sin/purification offering.
Priest shall make atonement/covering for soul/life who erred in sinning/misaligning in error before YHWH, to make covering for him, and it shall be forgiven him.
For native among children of Israel and for sojourner sojourning in their midst, one Torah/instruction shall be for you for one who acts in error.
But soul/life who acts with raised hand, from native or from sojourner, reviles YHWH; that soul/life shall be cut off from midst of its people.
Because word of YHWH he despised and his command he broke, that soul/life shall surely be cut off; its iniquity is upon it.
While children of Israel were in wilderness, they found a man gathering wood on Sabbath day.
Those who found him gathering wood brought him near to Moses and Aaron and all congregation.
They put him under guard, for it had not been made clear what should be done to him.
YHWH said to Moses, The man shall surely be put to death; all congregation shall stone him with stones outside camp.
All congregation brought him outside camp and stoned him with stones, and he died, as YHWH commanded Moses.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Speak to children of Israel and say to them that they shall make for themselves tassels on corners of their garments throughout generations, and put upon tassel of corner a cord of blue.
It shall be tassel for you, and you shall see it and remember all commands of YHWH and do them, and not scout after your heart and after your eyes, after which you go astray,
so that you remember and do all my commands and be holy to your Divine.
I am YHWH your Divine, who brought you out from land of Egypt to be to you as Divine. I am YHWH your Divine.

translation_notes: The chapter looks beyond wilderness judgment toward future settled life, offerings, bread, and remembrance.
Sojourner and native are placed under one statute and one instruction in worship and inadvertent-sin provisions; this must not be narrowed into ethnic exclusion.
First-dough contribution sanctifies ordinary food production and household labor.
The text distinguishes inadvertent error from deliberate raised-hand defiance.
The Sabbath wood-gatherer execution is severe ancient legal material. The holding period before judgment shows process, but the sentence must never authorize modern religious violence.
Tassels and blue cord are embodied memory aids, not markers of superiority or permission to police others' clothing.
Verse 39's 'scout after heart and eyes' echoes the scouting language of chapter 13 and redirects perception toward covenant remembrance.
safety_note: Do not use Numbers 15 to justify execution for Sabbath violation, vigilantism, xenophobia, legalistic domination, or policing religious dress. Preserve the ancient legal text while centering equal law, forgiveness for error, embodied remembrance, and Exodus liberation.
